—Bildad raises the question about the prosperity of wicked people, but points out how far apart God and humans are. He reminds Job of some truths that he has not fully considered. Even at their best, a person's righteousness and holiness mean nothing compared to God’s. Since God is so great and glorious, how can a guilty and impure human appear before him? We need to be born again by water and the Holy Spirit, and cleansed repeatedly in the blood of Christ, that Fountain opened. We should be humbled as insignificant, guilty, polluted creatures, and give up self-reliance. But our wickedness will show Christ’s kindness and love; the riches of his mercy and the power of his grace will be magnified to all eternity by every sinner he redeems.
